summary: Iteration 2 testing complete. All new optimizations verified: 24-chapter dataset, PDF buttons (home + drawer), in-memory cache prevents refetch on drawer open/chapter navigation, chapter 17 (11217 words / 2306 paragraphs) renders smoothly, chapter 24 is final with correct nav states. Backend 6/6 tests pass, frontend 8/8 checks pass.
passed: GET /api/novel/chapters -> 24 items, last is 'El primer paso'
GET /api/novel/chapters/17 -> 'El camino de las mil huellas', word_count=11217
Chapters tab shows 24 chapter-link-* items; click chapter-link-24 -> /read/24
/read/24 shows 'El primer paso', prev enabled, next disabled
Opening drawer on /read/1 triggers 0 API refetches (novelCache works)
drawer-pdf-btn visible above list, href=/historia-vol1.pdf target=_blank
Navigating via drawer-chapter-5 triggers ONLY /api/novel/chapters/5 (no meta/list refetch)
/read/17 renders 2306 paragraphs with paragraph-cv class; hero illustration uses loading='lazy'
No '@/' imports found in App.js/index.js/illustrations.js; build script remains 'craco build'
critical_code_review_comments: list_chapters() in server.py fetches illustrations from Mongo but never uses illus_set — dead code, safe to remove
Frontend novelCache is module-scope global; fine for single SPA session but resets on hard reload (expected behavior)
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 24-chapter + PDF + cache optimization features verified end-to-end. AI illustration generation was not re-tested (verified in iteration 1). Chapter 17 (largest, 11k words / 2306 paragraphs) renders without issues.
